T1.name: Direct sunlight / glare
T1.real_world_example: Morning/evening driving toward sun, tunnel exit

T2.name: Rain / LiDAR point dropout
T2.real_world_example: Moderate to heavy rainfall, wet road spray

T3.name: Combined degradation (glare + rain)
T3.real_world_example: Rainy day driving toward low sun

T4.name: Pedestrian crossing under sensor degradation
T4.real_world_example: Crosswalk in direct sunlight with rain

T5.name: Extreme combined sensor failure
T5.real_world_example: Snowstorm at night near tunnel exit

hara_table.hazard: False positive pedestrian detection — unnecessary braking
hara_table.safety_goal: Perception confidence shall not trigger planning decisions when camera reliability < 0.5
hara_table.mitigation: Loop 1: LiDAR trust weight increases; Loop 2: velocity reduces to cautious profile

hara_table.hazard: False negative — pedestrian missed in glare region
hara_table.safety_goal: System shall enter CONSERVATIVE regime when camera confidence drops below threshold at pedestrian-present affordance
hara_table.mitigation: Loop 1: LiDAR assumes primary role; Loop 2: CONSERVATIVE mode + CBF hard constraint

hara_table.hazard: Range estimation error — incorrect distance to leading vehicle
hara_table.safety_goal: TTC safety margin shall increase proportionally to LiDAR dropout rate
hara_table.mitigation: Loop 1: Camera range estimates weighted up; Loop 2: TTC margin increases from 2.0s to 4.5s

hara_table.hazard: Both sensors degraded — scene understanding unreliable
hara_table.safety_goal: System shall enter CONSERVATIVE mode when combined sensor reliability < 0.35
hara_table.mitigation: Regime classifier triggers CONSERVATIVE; K(t)=0.15 — slow control response; RSS hard constraints active

hara_table.hazard: Undetected pedestrian at crossing under combined sensor failure
hara_table.sotif_region: Unknown unsafe → Known unsafe via framework
hara_table.safety_goal: Pedestrian affordance risk shall override uncertainty thresholding — same uncertainty triggers stricter regime when pedestrian present
hara_table.mitigation: Affordance-mediated regime: pedestrian_risk × uncertainty → CONSERVATIVE regardless of individual thresholds

hara_table.hazard: Complete perception unreliability — no safe scene understanding possible
hara_table.safety_goal: System shall request MRC (Minimal Risk Condition) when both sensors below minimum threshold
hara_table.mitigation: K(t)=0.0 — freeze controls; MRC request; watchdog FSM triggers safe stop
